_comment: DC2 enemy data. THREE layers: (1) 'models' = the 13 external E*.DAT slots with obj/tri/quad counts decoded FROM BYTES (tools/dc2_re/dcm_and_misc.py). (2) 'roster' = the 11 distinct creatures (Velociraptor counted once) with size/role from FAQ + Wikipedia + Dino Crisis Wiki api.php. (3) 'mapping' = slot<->creature, now driven by RUNTIME live capture (see mapping.resolved_runtime + mapping.method_runtime): CONFIRMED LIVE E00=Velociraptor, E90=Oviraptor, E50=Inostrancevia, E60=Allosaurus, E10=Tyrannosaurus, E40=Giganotosaurus; EA0=Compsognathus by inference. The earlier 'E30/E31/E32 = Velociraptor triple' lock is RETRACTED (live raptors incl. HARD green are E00) -- see mapping.retracted. remaining unresolved: Plesiosaurus (aquatic) over E31/E32/E80/EA0 (E30=Mosasaurus, E70=Triceratops, E20=Pteranodon now RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-30 via the op-0x1a cross-species cave, K61/K62/K62c; the aquatic shared TYPEs 0x0a/0x0b/0x0c crash on the swap so their E-slots are still open), resolved one-by-one by reading live actor species base [+0x60] in a known-creature encounter (Dino Colosseum is the cleanest venue, or the cross-species cave in ST202). 'raptorVariants' details the four colour variants; live evidence says they share the E00 model (CLUT/param deltas), so Blue/Red 'Super' raptors are NOT separately the E30/E31/E32 files.
_provenance_legend: bytes = decoded from game files this session; confirmed = >=2 external sources agree; single-source = one external source; inference = reasoned guess, unproven; placeholder = unknown.
_byte_method: obj_cnt @ decompressed LZSS0 +0x14; tri_cnt/quad_cnt u16 @ +0x10/+0x12; ram_base from package entry reserve[0]. EA0 is the clear outlier (7 obj).
_hp_mechanism: [OFFLINE+LIVE 2026-06-24] Enemy HP = a hard-coded ctor immediate `mov word[actor+0x108],imm16` (`66 C7 86 08 01 00 00 <hp16>`; 108 sites in Dino2.exe rebirth). For NORMAL enemies the live HP EQUALS that constant (no scaling) - Colosseum-confirmed: Inostrancevia 2300 @0x0043754B (unique), Allosaurus 5000 @0x00425FD0|0x00439ACA, T-Rex max 10000=0x2710 + 0xFFFF cur-HP (x35 sites). NOTE [2026-07-06]: the 0xFFFF is NOT a checked invincibility sentinel -- there is NO `cmp [+0x108],0xFFFF` anywhere and as signed -1 it would read as already-dead; it is (a) the cur-HP written by the T-Rex scripted-intro/INVINCIBLE STATE (0x4235a8, table 0x720078[0]/entry 0x423410) and (b) a post-death dead-marker (e.g. 0x402cef). T-Rex killability = whether the boss reaches its FIGHTABLE state (0x4238d0, table 0x7201b0[0]) which sets finite HP 10000/20000/5000 + combat tables; the campaign randomizer-swap T-Rex is stuck in the intro/invincible state. See docs/dc2/DC2-TREX-KILLABILITY-RE.md. Randomizer HP lever (normal enemies) = patch the ctor immediate (unique values 300/600/1400/1500/2300/2800/3000/4600/5333/8000/15000/30000 each = one ctor). EXCEPTIONS: the RAPTOR (ctor 0x004461DE writes Brown base 1000; campaign-HARD Green 818 / Red 3252 are NOT ctor immediates and have no .data table -> colour+difficulty spawn param, scaler OPEN) and Oviraptor (live 424 is not a ctor immediate either). Colour is NOT an actor field (material const [+0x104]=0x7FC0009E identical green/red) -> CLUT bound at E00.TEX load. See docs/dc2/EXE-SPAWN-SYSTEM.md + KaQ K45, OPEN #12.

models.note: RESOLVED LIVE: E00 = Velociraptor. Live actors with species base 0x00633000 across THREE colours: ST102/ST105 Brown (HP ~818), ST201 HARD Green (HP ~818), and Facility/Front-Area HARD Red (HP ~3252, live slots 9-10) -- ALL are E00. â‡’ raptor colours are CLUT/tier deltas on the shared E00 model (HP scales with tier: green ~818 << red ~3252), NOT separate model files. This CONTRADICTS (triple-confirmed) the old guess that E30/E31/E32 = the Velociraptor triple (see mapping._status / mapping.retracted).

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-24: E10 = Tyrannosaurus (T-Rex). Dino Colosseum, T-Rex active (last colosseum enemy): live actor slot 13 species base [+0x60]=0x00640000 (SHARED). Disambiguated by RESIDENT model header at RAM 0x00640000: tri@+0x10=410, quad@+0x12=72, obj@+0x14=20 = EXACT unique match to E10. Live HP word-pair [+0x108]=0xFFFF (cur) / 0x2710=10000 (max). REFINED [2026-07-06]: 0xFFFF is the cur-HP of the T-Rex scripted-intro/invincible STATE, NOT a checked sentinel (no cmp against it exists). The T-Rex is a two-behavior-table boss: invincible-intro (0x423410, writes 0xFFFF @0x4235a8) vs fightable (0x4238d0, writes finite 10000 @0x423937 + installs combat tables). Campaign randomizer-swap T-Rex never transitions to the fightable state -> unkillable; Extra Crisis flow drives the transition -> killable. See docs/dc2/DC2-TREX-KILLABILITY-RE.md.

models.creature_confidence: confirmed (runtime live CE cave ST202 2026-06-30, K62c)
models.note: RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-30 (K62c): E20 = Pteranodon (FLYER), via spawn TYPE 0x04 (ctor 0x00429040). Live cave swap in ST202 loaded E20 (resident header 358/58/18 @0x640000) and spawned 3 actors, NO crash, HP 1200/2400 -> FLYER (crash-safe as a land spawn, unlike aquatic).

models.creature_confidence: high (LIVE ST704 2026-06-27: user visual ID + resident model header 290/132/20 = E30 at the actor model cluster 0x5E3878 AND canonical shared base 0x640010; matches obj20/tri290/quad132)
models.note: Part of the E30/E31/E32 triple = the Velociraptor colour-variant family (E31==E32 geometry). Which colour (Brown/Green/Red/Blue) maps to which file is unproven.
Part of the E30/E31/E32 triple = the Velociraptor colour-variant family (E31==E32 geometry). Which colour (Brown/Green/Red/Blue) maps to which file is unproven.
Part of the E30/E31/E32 triple = the Velociraptor colour-variant family (E31==E32 geometry). Which colour (Brown/Green/Red/Blue) maps to which file is unproven.

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-24: E40 = Giganotosaurus (FINAL BOSS). Captured in a late-game boss room with the Giganotosaurus active: boss actor species base [+0x60]=0x005E5044 (stage-specific load addr, NOT the Colosseum reserve 0x63f500); disambiguated by the RESIDENT model header at 0x005E5044 = tri@+0x10=570, quad@+0x12=69, obj@+0x14=20 = EXACT unique match to E40's byte counts (570 tri is unique among all 13 slots). Live HP=30000 (0x7530), final-boss tier. Player (base 0x00662500, HP 1200) + partner (base 0x00614E80) actors confirm array alignment; E40 was the SOLE enemy E-model resident. See mapping.resolved_runtime. NOTE: outside the Colosseum the unique-base self-identify shortcut does NOT hold (model loaded at 0x5E5044, not 0x63f500) -- the header counts are the robust key.

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-24: E50 = Inostrancevia. Dino Colosseum, Inostrancevia active: 3 live actors (slots 11-13) species base [+0x60]=0x00640000 (the SHARED base). Disambiguated by reading the RESIDENT model header at RAM 0x00640000: tri@+0x10=370, quad@+0x12=19, obj@+0x14=19 = EXACT unique match to E50's byte counts. Live HP=2300 (0x08FC). Validates the shared-base disambiguation method (resident-header read at 0x00640000).

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-24: E60 = Allosaurus. Dino Colosseum, 2 Allosaurus active: live actors (slots 12-13) species base [+0x60]=0x00638000 = the UNIQUE E60 reserve base (self-identifies, no resident-header read needed). Live HP=5000 (0x1388) -- tanky mini-boss, consistent with the guide's Allosaurus role.

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-30 (K62): E70 = Triceratops, via spawn TYPE 0x09 (ctor 0x0043cf10, unique dedicated base 0x650000). Live cave swap in ST202 loaded E70 (resident header 434/62/20 @0x650000) and spawned 3 land actors, NO crash, HP 5333 -> LAND. See mapping.type_ctor_map.resolved_unique_base.0x09.
One of the 9 unresolved non-raptor creatures (see mapping.unresolved). obj_cnt does not discriminate; needs the enemy-id<->E-file link.

RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-24: E90 = Oviraptor. Dino Colosseum with the Oviraptor enemy active: 4 live actors (slots 10-13, array base 0x5F3A14 stride 0x260) all species base [+0x60]=0x0063d000 (the unique E90 reserve base), [+0x5C]=0, instance ptrs [+0x64]=0x5FEDB0/5FE024/5FD298/5FC50C, live HP=424 (0x01A8 cur=max). 0x0063d000 is unique to E90 among all 13 slots â‡’ unambiguous.
Uniquely tiny rig (7 obj / 32 tri / 23 KB) - matches Compsognathus (~0.5 m). The only size-distinctive slot.

mapping._status: RUNTIME OVERRIDE (2026-06-24): live actor captures supersede the earlier count-balance inference. The old guess 'E30/E31/E32 = Velociraptor triple' is RETRACTED -- live raptors (Brown ST102/105, Green HARD ST201) are all E00, and the Dino Colosseum Oviraptor is E90. The within-set assignment is now being rebuilt empirically (visit a known-creature room/colosseum fight, read the live actor species base [+0x60]); guide+inference are corroborators only. obj_cnt (17-21) does NOT discriminate body type, so live capture is the authoritative method.
mapping.method_runtime: Attach CE to Dino2.exe; actor array = [[0x876DB8]+0x940], stride 0x260, count [0x876DC4]; per actor species model base = [+0x60] (resting; [+0x5C]=0), instance ptr [+0x64], live HP word-pair near [+0x108..0x10C]. Match [+0x60] to a unique ram_base in models[] (E00/E40/E60/E70/E90 have unique bases). For the 0x00640000-shared files (E10/E20/E30/E31/E32/E80/EA0), read the RESIDENT model header at RAM 0x00640000 -- tri u16 @ +0x10, quad u16 @ +0x12, obj u32 @ +0x14 -- and match models[] counts (validated: E50 read 370/19/19). Only one 0x640000-base model is resident at a time, so in a single-species fight this is unambiguous EXCEPT the E31/E32 pair (identical 214/135 -> needs the CreateFileA filename hook K32). See docs/dc2/EXE-SPAWN-SYSTEM.md.

type_ctor_map._summary: STATIC 2026-06-25 (K49): the spawn-opcode TYPE byte (op 0x1a block[+0x20] -> [actor+0x58]) indexes the per-type ctor dispatch table at 0x731D98 (= live [0x876C60], K34). Each ctor's body contains its model-base load; base -> E-file (models[].ram_base) -> creature. Tool: tools/dc2_re/type_species.py (reproducible). Unique bases resolve 1:1; the shared 0x640000 group needs the per-room CreateFileA filename (K32) or live capture to split. NOTE: TYPE is a BEHAVIOR/ctor class, not always 1:1 with species -- TYPE 0x10 is a GENERIC spawn whose model comes from the spawn record's MODEL_BASE field (block[+0x0c], often a global), so the same TYPE 0x10 spawns different species across rooms (it is the dominant spawn path, e.g. all of ST300 and ST102 routine-1).
type_ctor_map._hp_note: hp_cur = ctor `mov word[+0x108]` (matches live), hp_max = `[+0x10a]` (~2x). Cross-validated: 0x06=30000 == K46 LIVE Gigano; 0x08=5000 == K45 Allosaurus; 0x0e=2300 == K45 Inostrancevia.

0x09.note: RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-30 (K62, CE cave on op-0x1a hook 0x487b76, donor poke 0x02->0x09 in ST202): loaded category [scene_mgr+0x680]=9; resident model header @0x650000 = tri 434/quad 62/obj 20 = EXACT unique E70 match; 3 live actors (slots 5/6/7) [+0x60]=0x650000, [+0x58]=9, HP-max [+0x10a]=5333 (=ctor immediate). NO CRASH => LAND => Triceratops (maintainer in-game visual ID). LAND + dedicated base + non-boss => an eligible cross-species donor (parity with Allosaurus 5000). Corrects an earlier erroneous 'Plesiosaurus/aquatic' note (retracted).

shared_640000_group._note: base 0x00640000 = E10/E20/E30/E31/E32/E50/E80/EA0. HP-split is reliable ONLY when an HP value is UNIQUE among the shared TYPEs AND live-confirmed. Resolved: 0x0e=Inostrancevia(E50) via unique HP 2300 (live ✓ ST202 swap). **0x03/0x05 RESOLVED by the live cross-species swap (2026-06-30) + the residency table (cat→file 0x703520): 0x03=E10 Tyrannosaurus (live ✓), 0x05=E30 Mosasaurus (live ✗ CRASH — aquatic).** The old 'both 0x10000-HP => both T-Rex' guess is RETRACTED: they share HP but are different creatures (the file loaded is keyed by category, not the ctor HP). Rest need the per-room CreateFileA E-file trace (K32) or live capture.
shared_640000_group._habitat_note: HABITAT RULE refined by the K62/K62c live cave swaps (2026-06-30): only AQUATIC creatures CRASH when forced into a land room (their ctor resolves a NULL anim-clip / assumes a water scene — 0x05 Mosasaurus and 0x0c both crashed with a NULL+5 read at anim setter 0x48DE55). LAND and FLYER spawn FINE — the Pteranodon (0x04/E20, FLYER) spawned with NO crash, refuting the earlier 'flyers also crash' guess. Cross-room donors must be LAND (flyers excluded too: ground-AI unverified). Land live-confirmed: 0x02 Raptor, 0x06 Gigano, 0x07 Ovi, 0x08 Allo, 0x0e Inostrancevia, 0x03 T-Rex, 0x09 Triceratops(E70). Flyer: 0x04 Pteranodon(E20). Aquatic (crash): 0x05 Mosasaurus(E30), 0x0a (user-ID aquatic), 0x0b, 0x0c (crashed = aquatic; E-slots open).

0x03.confidence: KNOWN (live ✓ ST202 swap 2026-06-30; cat3->E10 in residency table 0x703520)

0x05.confidence: KNOWN (live ✗ CRASH ST202 2026-06-30 = aquatic; cat5->E30 in residency table 0x703520)

0x04.note: RESOLVED LIVE 2026-06-30 (K62c, cross-species cave donor poke 0x02->0x04 in ST202): loaded category [scene_mgr+0x680]=4; resident model header @0x640000 = tri 358/quad 58/obj 18 = EXACT unique E20 match; 3 live actors (slots 5/6/7) [+0x58]=4, [+0x60]=0x640000, HP-max [+0x10a]=2400 (=ctor immediate). Maintainer in-game ID = Pteranodon (flying dino). NO CRASH ⇒ FLYER is crash-SAFE as a land spawn (refutes the prior 'flyers also crash' assumption — only AQUATIC crashes). Native host: shared 0x640000 group. NOT a default donor (flyer ground-AI unverified).

0x0a.confidence: habitat KNOWN (live CRASH + maintainer in-game visual ID = AQUATIC, ST202 2026-06-30, K62b); creature/E-slot open
0x0a.note: LIVE 2026-06-30 (cross-species cave, donor poke 0x02->0x0a in ST202): CRASHED on the fresh spawn; maintainer identified it as an AQUATIC enemy. Native host room ST700 (survey). Same crash bucket as Mosasaurus 0x05. Never a land donor; specific creature/E-slot still open (aquatic candidates: Plesiosaurus, or a second Mosasaurus-class).

0x0b.confidence: habitat KNOWN (live CRASH ST202 2026-06-30, K62b); creature/E-slot open
0x0b.note: LIVE 2026-06-30 (cross-species cave, donor poke 0x02->0x0b in ST202): CRASHED on the fresh spawn (no dump generated) => NON-LAND (aquatic/flyer), same constraint bucket as Mosasaurus 0x05 and 0x0c. Never a cross-species donor; aquatic-vs-flyer needs a live capture in its native stage.

0x0c.confidence: habitat KNOWN (live CRASH ST202 2026-06-30, K62b); creature/E-slot still open
0x0c.note: LIVE 2026-06-30 (cross-species cave, donor poke 0x02->0x0c in ST202): CRASHED on the fresh spawn => NON-LAND (aquatic/flyer), same constraint bucket as Mosasaurus 0x05. RCA of dump 'DC2 2026-06-30 18-08-16.dmp': access-violation 0xC0000005, NULL+5 read at 0x48DE55 in the anim-clip setter SetAnimClip 0x48DE40 (the actor's resident animation-clip pointer was NULL during ctor anim setup — the creature's scene-specific anim set is absent from a land room). EA0/Compy candidate RULED OUT (Compy is land; HP 1200/2400 also not the tiny rig). Aquatic-vs-flyer NOT distinguishable from the null-clip signature — needs a live capture in its native water/air stage. So 0x0c is NEVER a cross-species donor.

type_ctor_map.survey: tools/dc2_re/survey_types.py tallies TYPE usage across all 89 rooms. KEY: TYPE 0x10 (generic, model-from-record) dominates (42 rooms); species-hardcoded TYPEs are rare (0x06 Gigano: ST402/ST503; 0x07 Ovi: ST302; 0x08 Allo: ST100/ST10C/ST802/ST808; 0x02 Raptor: ST102/ST407; 0x09 E70: ST80A; shared 0x03: ST200/ST903, 0x05: ST706, 0x0a: ST700, 0x0e: ST80B). Room->creature naming blocked: stage->zone is an unproven count-match (ROOM-ZONE-CLUSTERING.md), so room context can't name the shared creatures statically.

block_base_types._note: no hardcoded model base; model comes from the spawn record (block[+0x0c]) or non-enemy.
block_base_types.0x00: player (HP 1200; live base 0x662500, K46 Gigano-room actor 0)
block_base_types.0x01: partner / 2P (HP 1200; live base 0x614E80, K46 Gigano-room actor 3)

0x10.note: GENERIC enemy spawn; model from block[+0x0c] (E00 raptor in ST102 r1 via global; species varies per room). Dominant spawn path.

block_base_types.0x11-0x24: non-enemy actors (effects/items/NPC); no model base, no HP ctor immediate

type_ctor_map.randomizer_note: Two swap levers: (1) for species-hardcoded TYPEs (0x02/0x06/0x07/0x08/0x09/0x0d + the 0x640000 group) edit the op-0x1a TYPE literal (block[+0x20]) in slot-5; (2) for TYPE-0x10 generic spawns edit the MODEL_BASE operand (block[+0x0c]) -- but it is often a global-ref, not a literal.

resolved_runtime.base: 0x005E5044 (this capture; stage-specific) -- NOT the Colosseum E40 reserve 0x0063f500: enemy models load at stage-dependent RAM addresses, so the base value alone does NOT self-identify E40 outside the Colosseum. The model-intrinsic header counts are the robust key.
resolved_runtime.basis: Late-game boss room, active Giganotosaurus on screen. Actor array [[0x876DB8]+0x940]=0x005FB9FC, stride 0x260, count [0x876DC4]=31. The boss actor (slot 1 @0x005FBC5C, HP 30000, instance ptr [+0x64]=0x00603974) has species base [+0x60]=0x005E5044. Disambiguated by reading the RESIDENT model header at RAM 0x005E5044: tri@+0x10=570, quad@+0x12=69, obj@+0x14=20 = EXACT unique match to E40's byte counts (570 tri / 69 quad are unique among all 13 slots). Array alignment cross-checked: slot 0 player (base 0x00662500, HP 1200 = K43's recorded player base) + a partner/NPC (base 0x00614E80, header 362/153/15 = matches NO E-file); E40 (0x005E5044) is the SOLE enemy E-model resident in the room. A second slot-2 actor also carries base 0x005E5044 (HP 1000, inst=0) = boss secondary part / inactive E40 instance.
resolved_runtime.hp: 30000 (0x7530). A unique value in the _hp_mechanism / K45 ctor-immediate list => the E40 (Giganotosaurus) HP-edit lever is the lone `mov word[actor+0x108],0x7530` site.

resolved_runtime.evidence: LIVE ST704 (CE 2026-06-27, PID 8296): user visual ID + byte-grounded resident header (tri290/quad132/obj20 @ model base 0x5E3878, also @0x640010). Spawned via generic TYPE 0x13 (model-from-global). E31/E32 (214/135/17) are DIFFERENT geometry -> the old E30/E31/E32 'one-creature triple' is split: E30=Mosasaurus, E31/E32=separate creature (open).

locked.basis: only uniquely-tiny rig (7 obj / 32 tri / 23 KB) vs Compy's ~0.5 m size; not yet runtime-confirmed but consistent

retracted.old_basis: the ONLY multi-variant enemy maps to the ONLY triple-file group (count-balance inference)
retracted.why_retracted: Live raptors (incl. the HARD green) are E00, not E30/E31/E32. The raptor is E00. What E30/E31/E32 actually are is now UNKNOWN (a different creature with sub-variants -- candidates: a Pteranodon/large-theropod colour set, or Red/Blue 'Super' raptor variants distinct from the E00 base). Needs live capture.

unresolved.note: Mosasaurus RESOLVED = E30 (live ST704). Remaining 3 creatures across files {E20,E70,E80,[E31/E32]}. E31/E32 (214/135/17, identical geometry) = one creature with a sub-variant; E30 split off from the old triple. Resolve each by live capture (room + visual + resident-header read).
unresolved.how_to_resolve: Live actor species-base capture per known-creature encounter; read the resident model header at whatever address [+0x60] points to (NOT a fixed reserve base -- see the E40 off-Colosseum caveat) and match models[] counts. The Dino Colosseum roster (Velociraptor/Oviraptor/Inostrancevia/Allosaurus/T-Rex) is the cleanest venue and is now exhausted for the base set; Giganotosaurus resolved via its late-game campaign boss room. Remaining Pteranodon (3rd Energy Pathway 2), Plesiosaurus (3rd Energy Dock), Mosasaurus (3rd Energy Reactor Elevator Shaft) are campaign aquatic/aerial grunts; Triceratops via colosseum-unlock or campaign set-piece.
unresolved.status_after_exe_check: EXE id table has no creature names (see mapping.exe_id_table); resolution is by runtime capture, IN PROGRESS (6 of the set done: E00=Velociraptor, E90=Oviraptor, E50=Inostrancevia, E60=Allosaurus, E10=Tyrannosaurus, E40=Giganotosaurus in resolved_runtime).

exe_id_table.location: Dino2.exe master filename pointer table @ VA 0x0071b3xx (.data; imagebase 0x400000). Tool: tools/dc2_re/exe_enemy_table.py.
exe_id_table.e_file_order: E00,E10,E20,E30,E31,E32,E40,E50,E60,E70,E80,E90,EA0 (the on-disk 13; id == high nibble of the E-file number, with E30/E31/E32 = type 3 + two subtypes).
exe_id_table.what_it_confirms: id <-> E-file (the enemy id table). The consecutive E30/E31/E32 triple corroborates the Velociraptor colour-variant lock; EA0 (type 0x0A) is the lone outlier = Compsognathus.
exe_id_table.what_it_does_NOT_contain: Creature names. All 11 species strings (Velociraptor, Allosaurus, Oviraptor, Inostrancevia, Triceratops, Giganotosaurus, Compsognathus, Mosasaurus, Plesiosaurus, Pteranodon, Tyrannosaurus) AND 'Raptor' = 0 hits in the EXE. The table is id->FILENAME, never id->creature.
exe_id_table.conclusion: The EXE enemy-id table CANNOT resolve the within-9 file->creature assignment by itself - it has no species labels. Resolution still requires the in-room spawn-record decode (docs/dc2/SCRIPT-DC2.md) or a runtime RAM dump of a room with a known enemy. Per-room enemy lists are NOT recoverable from the room sound-config DATA blobs (only ST503 carries T=3 records, and those are stride-16 sound indices, not E-file ids).

Mosasaurus.source: CE live actor read + user visual ID 2026-06-27
Mosasaurus.note: First location of Mosasaurus (one of the 4 long-open creatures). Confirms it spawns via generic TYPE 0x13, so species is room-E-file-driven not TYPE-driven.
